science.title: A grad student’s wild idea sparks a major aging breakthrough
science.summary: A casual conversation between graduate students helped spark a breakthrough in aging research at Mayo Clinic. Researchers discovered that tiny synthetic DNA molecules called aptamers can selectively attach to senescent “zombie cells,” which are linked to aging, cancer, and neurodegenerative disease. science.title: Earth is flying through ancient supernova debris and scientists found the evidence in Antarctic ice
science.summary: Earth is quietly collecting radioactive debris from an ancient stellar explosion as our Solar System drifts through a giant cloud of gas and dust between the stars. science.title: Scientists make old blood stem cells young again in major anti-aging breakthrough
science.summary: As blood stem cells age, their lysosomes become overactive and damaged, triggering inflammation and weakening the body’s ability to regenerate healthy blood and immune cells. science.title: Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones
science.summary: Scientists have uncovered compelling evidence that dinosaur fossils may still contain traces of their original proteins, overturning a long-standing belief that fossilization destroys all organic material. science.title: Who are the Japanese? Huge DNA discovery rewrites history
science.summary: Scientists analyzing the genomes of thousands of people across Japan discovered evidence for a previously overlooked third ancestral group, challenging the long-accepted “dual origins” theory. medicine.title: New targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy induces remission in pancreatic cancer model
medicine.summary: A newly developed targeted radiopharmaceutical treatment can effectively slow tumor growth in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), according to new research published in the May issue of The Journal of Nuclear Medicine. medicine.title: Novel CAR T cell therapy moves into clinical studies
medicine.summary: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center and CTMC, a joint venture between UT MD Anderson and Resilience, today announced that the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued a 'safe to proceed' for the Investigational New Drug (IND) application for a novel chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell therapy.

medicine.title: Improving the treatment of Lassa fever: First clinical trial with favipiravir shows promising results
medicine.summary: The infectious disease Lassa fever can be fatal, and there is a lack of treatment options with proven efficacy. astronomy.title: How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form?
astronomy.summary: A planetary odd couple — a mini-Neptune and a hot Jupiter — probably formed much farther away from their star before migrating closer in. The post How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: We've Been Wasting 99% of Our Supernova Data
astronomy.summary: Every time an astronomer points a telescope at a distant supernova, they're trying to measure how far away it is.
